note: Radio uses RFCOMM/SPP-like framing over L2CAP dynamic channels, not standard BLE GATT. The 0xAB protocol runs inside the L2CAP payload.

txFrames.note: This capture only shows 2 commands — user was likely just connecting and pressing Band once

0x01.purpose: Status update type 1 — contains nested 0xAB frames

0x10.purpose: Frequency display data — the current frequency digits

0x17.purpose: UI state — button enable/disable (confirmed from source code)

0x19.purpose: Extended status — contains multiple nested frames

0x1C.purpose: PRIMARY DATA STREAM — main radio status feed (signal strength, tuning, mode)

protocolInsights.nestedFrames: Response group 0x01 and 0x19 contain nested 0xAB frames — the radio sends compound status updates with multiple sub-messages in one notification
protocolInsights.primaryDataStream: Group 0x1C accounts for 73% of all responses — this is the continuous radio status feed that drives the UI display
protocolInsights.frequencyUpdates: Group 0x10 with 44 frames shows frequency updates — this fires whenever the tuning changes
protocolInsights.lowCommandCount: Only 2 TX commands in this capture suggests the user connected and pressed Band once — a fuller capture with frequency entry, volume changes, and mode switching would reveal more command patterns
